reason: Predates domain-content decision audit; contains unclassified/uncorrected placeholder peak-volume and dosage values, and was published before the PLACEHOLDER_UNCONFIRMED publish guard existed. See artifacts/audits/ten-k-pilot-domain-decision-audit.md.

Superseded by 0.2.0-pilot after TEN_K/INTERMEDIATE/4D domain-content reconciliation against Golden Fixture v3 (PrescriptionMode/DistanceAccountingMode vocabulary correction, 4 workout definitions upgraded to CANONICAL_CONFIRMED, phase preferred-weeks and peak-volume corroborated by a second source).
Replaced by 0.3.0-pilot, which includes the canonical TEN_K_MASTER v2 TAPER phase-family eligibility correction.

0.3.0-pilot's release manifest mislabeled TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE combination version 1 with content that actually referenced TEN_K_MASTER v2 (a source-side immutability defect: the v1 combination file had been mutated in place instead of being properly versioned). 0.4.0-pilot corrects this: combination v1 is restored to its exact historical content and combination v2 is a distinct, correctly-versioned artifact referencing TEN_K_MASTER v2.

Replaced by 0.5.0-pilot, which remediates 5 previously-undiscovered in-place mutations (4 WORKOUT_DEFINITION v1 artifacts and PEAK_VOLUME_BAND_POLICY v1) found by the repository-wide cross-release hash consistency scan. 0.4.0-pilot itself did not contain these mutations differently than earlier releases (it inherited them unchanged from 0.2.0-pilot/0.1.0-pilot) but is superseded because 0.5.0-pilot is now the corrected active pilot release, pinning combination v3 (RulePack v2, peak-volume-policy v2, workout v2s).

Replaced by 0.6.0-pilot, which completes the deterministic dependency-graph migration (Parts 1-3): TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE v1/v2/v3 retired from new-release eligibility (ledger-only, no source mutation); v4 (exact-versioned dependency graph, self-contained union workout closure including LONG_RUN_STANDARD) is now the sole active root. See artifacts/audits/deterministic-graph-part3-completion.md.
